title: Canonical CI validation workflow hardening
goal: Ensure the canonical CI workflow always runs the same four validation commands in the same order as local verification
scope: Harden canonical GitHub Actions validation workflow
Assert exact command order for pytest, ruff, mypy, compileall
Add focused tests or fixtures for workflow contract if test coverage exists for CI docs alignment
constraints: do not add external secrets requirements
do not change local command semantics
allowed_changes_detail: docs/global_rules.md::CI canonical path rule section only (do not modify session JSON required keys rule added by session-107)
forbidden_changes: non-canonical workflows except references needed to clarify canonical status
acceptance_criteria: AC-108-01: validation.yml contains pytest, ruff, mypy, compileall in canonical order [test_validation_workflow_uses_canonical_command_order]
AC-108-02: workflow commands match documented canonical commands [test_validation_workflow_matches_documented_commands]
AC-108-03: validation.yml remains the canonical workflow path [test_validation_workflow_is_canonical_path]
AC-108-04: runtime code is unchanged in this session [manual_changed_files_check]
AC-108-05: local 4-command verification still passes after workflow changes [manual_4_command_verification]
